cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about the lines of the film "Elephant" and who created them. The options are A through D. First, I need to recall the relevant part of the text. The article says: "American director Gus Van Sant had no ready-made lines. The student actors made up their own dialogue, with Van Sant asking them to base their characters on their own lives." So the key points here are that there were no pre-written lines. The student actors themselves created their own dialogue. The director didn't give them lines; instead, he had them base their characters on their own lives. This points towards option A, which states that the lines were created by the student actors. Let me check the other options just to be sure. Option B says the lines were given by the director, but that contradicts the text. Option C mentions professional people designing the lines, but the text specifically says the students made up their own.
